question: [File: 'src/contracts/src/TorusCreateAndStake.sol -> Scope: Critical reward extraction or unfair reward access by an unprivileged attacker'] [Function: earlyEndStake + claim] Can an unprivileged attacker who holds two concurrent stake positions call earlyEndStake on position A (after 50% elapsed) to remove position A's shares from totalShares[startDay+i] for all remaining days, thereby inflating the per-share reward for position B which spans those same days, then call claim on position B at maturity to extract more TORUS than their fair entitlement? Preconditions: attacker creates two overlapping stakeTorus positions on the same startDayIndex with large torusAmount and stakingDays=88; position A is early-ended after 44 days. Call sequence: stakeTorus(largeAmount, 88) -> stakeTorus(largeAmount, 88) -> warp 44 days -> earlyEndStake(indexA) -> warp 44 more days -> claim(indexB). Invariant tested: sum of all minted rewards across all claimants must not exceed sum of rewardPool[d] + penaltiesInRewardPool[d] for all days d. Scoped impact: attacker mints excess TORUS rewards beyond their proportional share entitlement. Local proof idea: fork test on pinned block 25530134, deploy two overlapping stakes, early-end one, assert claim(indexB) mints strictly more than (rewardPool[day] * sharesB) / originalTotalShares[day] for each day.

[File: 'src/contracts/src/TorusCreateAndStake.sol -> Scope: Critical reward extraction or unfair reward access by an unprivileged attacker'] [Function: _checkAndUpdateRewardPool + claim] Can an unprivileged attacker who is the sole staker on currentDay capture all accumulated rewards from multiple consecutive empty days (totalShares[d]==0) that are rolled into penaltiesInRewardPool[currentDay] by _checkAndUpdateRewardPool, by timing their createTorus call to be the first interaction after a long gap with no stakers? Preconditions: no stakers exist for days D through D+N; attacker calls createTorus on day D+N+1 triggering _checkAndUpdateRewardPool which rolls rewardPool[d] for all empty days into penaltiesInRewardPool[D+N+1]; attacker is the only holder of shares on day D+N+1. Call sequence: warp N days with no interactions -> createTorus(power, 1) -> warp 1 day -> claim(0). Invariant tested: penaltiesInRewardPool[currentDay] must only contain penalties from late/early claims, not unclaimed rewards from days where totalShares[d]==0 that the attacker did not contribute to. Scoped impact: attacker extracts TORUS rewards accumulated over N days of protocol operation without having staked during those days. Local proof idea: unit test with N=30 empty days, single attacker stake on day 31, assert claimed rewards equal only day-31 rewardPool share, not the rolled-up empty-day rewards.

[File: 'src/contracts/src/TorusCreateAndStake.sol -> Scope: Critical reward extraction or unfair reward access by an unprivileged attacker'] [Function: _checkAndUpdateRewardPool] Does the while loop 'while (lastFilled > 0 && rewardPool[lastFilled] == 0) { lastFilled--; }' incorrectly skip the lastFilled day when totalShares[lastFilled] == 0, causing rewardPool[lastFilled] to never be redistributed to penaltiesInRewardPool, permanently locking those rewards away from all stakers? Preconditions: rewardPool[D] != 0 but totalShares[D] == 0 (no stakers on day D); _checkAndUpdateRewardPool is called on day D+1. Call sequence: createTorus on day D-1 -> stake expires -> no interaction on day D -> createTorus on day D+1 triggering _checkAndUpdateRewardPool. Invariant tested: every non-zero rewardPool[d] with totalShares[d]==0 must eventually be redistributed to penaltiesInRewardPool for some future day. Scoped impact: protocol rewards are permanently lost rather than redistributed, reducing total claimable rewards for legitimate stakers. Local proof idea: unit test asserting that after _checkAndUpdateRewardPool runs on day D+1, sum(rewardPool[d] for all d where totalShares[d]==0) == 0.

[File: 'src/contracts/src/TorusCreateAndStake.sol -> Scope: Critical reward extraction or unfair reward access by an unprivileged attacker'] [Function: earlyEndStake + claim (cross-position)] Can an unprivileged attacker call earlyEndStake on a large-share position to push (totalRewards - partialRewards) / 2 into penaltiesInRewardPool[todayIndex + 1], then immediately claim a second position whose stake spans todayIndex+1 and captures that penalty pool, extracting more TORUS than the second position's proportional share entitlement? Preconditions: attacker has two positions: position A (large, 88 days) and position B (small, 2 days starting on todayIndex); position A is early-ended at 50% elapsed so claimablePct=0 and the full totalRewards/2 goes to penaltiesInRewardPool[todayIndex+1]; position B matures on todayIndex+1. Call sequence: createTorus(maxPower, 88) -> stakeTorus(amount, 2) -> warp 44 days -> earlyEndStake(indexA) -> warp 1 day -> claim(indexB). Invariant tested: penaltiesInRewardPool[d] must be distributed proportionally to all shares on day d, not extractable by a single attacker who manufactured the penalty. Scoped impact: attacker extracts TORUS from the penalty pool disproportionate to their share of totalShares[todayIndex+1]. Local proof idea: fork test asserting claim(indexB) rewards <= (penaltiesInRewardPool[todayIndex+1] * sharesB) / totalShares[todayIndex+1].

[File: 'src/contracts/src/TorusCreateAndStake.sol -> Scope: Critical reward extraction or unfair reward access by an unprivileged attacker'] [Function: claimBatch] Can an unprivileged attacker pass the same stakeIndex twice in the stakeIndexes array to claimBatch, causing the first iteration to set claimedCreate=true and claimedStake=true and accumulate principal+rewards into totalMintAmount, and the second iteration to revert with AlreadyClaimed, rolling back the entire batch and leaving the position unclaimed, or alternatively does the batch mint before the revert check allowing double-mint? Preconditions: attacker has a matured createTorus position at index 0; calls claimBatch([0, 0]). Call sequence: createTorus(power, days) -> warp days -> claimBatch([0, 0]). Invariant tested: torus.mint must be called at most once per stake position across all claim paths. Scoped impact: double-minting of principal+rewards for a single position. Local proof idea: unit test asserting torus.totalSupply() increases by exactly principal+rewards after claimBatch([0,0]), not 2*(principal+rewards).

[File: 'src/contracts/src/TorusCreateAndStake.sol -> Scope: Critical reward extraction or unfair reward access by an unprivileged attacker'] [Function: stakeTorus / _distributeFees] Can an unprivileged attacker call stakeTorus with a torusAmount so small that the computed power = (torusAmount * difficulty * 100) / partialForLength rounds down to zero, resulting in costTitanX = 0 and _distributeFees(0) being called, allowing the attacker to stake TORUS and earn rewards without paying any TitanX or ETH fee? Preconditions: torusAmount = 1 wei TORUS; difficulty is at minimum (1e18); stakingDays chosen to maximize partialForLength. Call sequence: approve(torusCreateAndStake, 1) -> stakeTorus(1, 88) with msg.value=0. Invariant tested: every stakeTorus call must transfer a non-zero fee in TitanX or ETH proportional to the staked amount. Scoped impact: attacker earns staking rewards with zero cost, extracting TORUS minted by the protocol. Local proof idea: unit test with torusAmount=1, assert costTitanX > 0 or transaction reverts.

[File: 'src/contracts/src/TorusCreateAndStake.sol -> Scope: Critical reward extraction or unfair reward access by an unprivileged attacker'] [Function: earlyEndStake] Can an unprivileged attacker call earlyEndStake at exactly the 50% elapsed mark (elapsed == totalDuration / 2) where claimablePct = (50-50)*2 = 0, receiving their full principal back but 0 rewards, while still removing their shares from totalShares[startDay+daysElapsed..stakingDays-1], thereby inflating per-share rewards for all other stakers on those days at zero cost to the attacker? Preconditions: attacker has a stakeTorus position with stakingDays=88; exactly 44 days have elapsed. Call sequence: stakeTorus(largeAmount, 88) -> warp 44 days -> earlyEndStake(0). Invariant tested: totalShares[d] must not decrease unless the corresponding staker forfeits proportional rewards; share removal must be accompanied by reward forfeiture that benefits the pool, not a free gift to other stakers. Scoped impact: attacker recovers full principal with zero reward loss while gifting inflated rewards to colluding stakers on future days. Local proof idea: unit test asserting that after earlyEndStake at 50%, totalShares[startDay+44..87] decreases by stakePos.shares and penaltiesInRewardPool[todayIndex+1] increases by totalRewards/2.

[File: 'src/contracts/src/TorusCreateAndStake.sol -> Scope: Critical reward extraction or unfair reward access by an unprivileged attacker'] [Function: _checkAndUpdateRewardPool] Is there a scenario where _checkAndUpdateRewardPool adds rewardPool[d] (as nextPool) to penaltiesInRewardPool[currentDay] for an empty day d, but rewardPool[d] is also still readable by calculateStakingRewards for any staker whose stake spans day d (even though totalShares[d]==0 causes a skip), creating an accounting discrepancy where the same reward value is counted in both penaltiesInRewardPool[currentDay] and rewardPool[d] simultaneously? Preconditions: day d has totalShares[d]==0; _checkAndUpdateRewardPool is called on day currentDay > d. Call sequence: no stakers on day d -> createTorus on day currentDay -> calculateStakingRewards for a position spanning day d. Invariant tested: for any day d with totalShares[d]==0, rewardPool[d] must not contribute to any staker's reward calculation after being rolled into penaltiesInRewardPool. Scoped impact: double-counting of rewards allows stakers on currentDay to extract more TORUS than the protocol allocated. Local proof idea: invariant test asserting sum(rewardPool[d] for d where totalShares[d]>0) + sum(penaltiesInRewardPool[d] for all d) == INITIAL_DAILY_REWARD * decay_factor_sum.

[File: 'src/contracts/src/TorusCreateAndStake.sol -> Scope: Critical reward extraction or unfair reward access by an unprivileged attacker'] [Function: claim] Can an unprivileged attacker who has a matured createTorus position (claimedCreate=false, claimedStake=false) call claim to receive principal+rewards via torus.mint, and then call claim again on the same index, since after the first call claimedCreate=true and claimedStake=true, the check 'if (stakePos.claimedStake && stakePos.claimedCreate) revert AlreadyClaimed()' fires, but is there any path where the flags are set inconsistently allowing a second mint? Preconditions: attacker has a matured createTorus position. Call sequence: createTorus(power, days) -> warp days -> claim(0) -> claim(0). Invariant tested: torus.mint(msg.sender, totalAmount) must be called at most once per position index per user. Scoped impact: double-minting of principal+rewards. Local proof idea: unit test asserting second claim(0) reverts with AlreadyClaimed and totalSupply unchanged.

[File: 'src/contracts/src/TorusCreateAndStake.sol -> Scope: Critical reward extraction or unfair reward access by an unprivileged attacker'] [Function: claim + earlyEndStake] Can an unprivileged attacker call earlyEndStake on a stakeTorus position (claimedCreate=true, claimedStake=false) to receive principal + partialRewards, and then call claim on the same index, since earlyEndStake sets claimedStake=true but claim checks 'if (stakePos.claimedStake && stakePos.claimedCreate) revert AlreadyClaimed()', allowing the second call to be blocked, or is there a state where claim proceeds and mints additional rewards? Preconditions: attacker has a stakeTorus position at 75% elapsed. Call sequence: stakeTorus(amount, 88) -> warp 66 days -> earlyEndStake(0) -> warp 22 days -> claim(0). Invariant tested: a position with claimedStake=true must never have torus.mint called again for any reward amount. Scoped impact: double-extraction of staking rewards. Local proof idea: unit test asserting claim(0) after earlyEndStake(0) reverts with AlreadyClaimed.

[File: 'src/contracts/src/TorusCreateAndStake.sol -> Scope: Critical reward extraction or unfair reward access by an unprivileged attacker'] [Function: calculateStakingRewards] Does calculateStakingRewards iterate over stakePos.stakingDays using stakePos.startDayIndex as the base, and if _checkAndUpdateRewardPool has not been called for recent days (rewardPool[d]==0 for d in [startDayIndex, startDayIndex+stakingDays)), does the function return 0 rewards even for a fully matured position, allowing an attacker to observe this and front-run legitimate claimants by calling _checkAndUpdateRewardPool (via any state-changing function) to populate rewardPool before claiming, while other users who call claim without triggering the update receive 0 rewards? Preconditions: no interaction with the contract for 30 days; a legitimate staker's position has matured. Call sequence: attacker calls createTorus(1, 1) to trigger _checkAndUpdateRewardPool -> victim calls claim(victimIndex) in same block. Invariant tested: calculateStakingRewards must return the same value regardless of whether _checkAndUpdateRewardPool was called by the claimant or a third party. Scoped impact: victim receives correct rewards (no extraction), but this tests whether reward calculation is order-dependent. Local proof idea: unit test asserting claim() always calls _checkAndUpdateRewardPool before calculateStakingRewards.

[File: 'src/contracts/src/TorusCreateAndStake.sol -> Scope: Critical reward extraction or unfair reward access by an unprivileged attacker'] [Function: earlyEndStake] In the shares-removal loop 'for (uint8 i = uint8(daysElapsed); i < stakePos.stakingDays; i++)', if daysElapsed is 0 (attacker calls earlyEndStake on the same calendar day as stake creation, after 50% of a 2-day stake has elapsed), does uint8(0) correctly start the loop, and does totalShares[startDay + 0] -= stakePos.shares underflow if another earlyEndStake or expiry already removed those shares? Preconditions: stakingDays=2; attacker calls earlyEndStake after exactly 1 day (50% elapsed); daysElapsed = getCurrentDayIndex() - startDayIndex = 0 if called within the same day. Call sequence: stakeTorus(amount, 2) -> warp 12 hours -> earlyEndStake(0). Invariant tested: totalShares[d] must never underflow below zero for any day d. Scoped impact: underflow would corrupt totalShares accounting, allowing subsequent stakers to claim inflated rewards. Local proof idea: unit test asserting totalShares[startDay] >= stakePos.shares before earlyEndStake executes the removal loop.

[File: 'src/contracts/src/TorusCreateAndStake.sol -> Scope: Critical reward extraction or unfair reward access by an unprivileged attacker'] [Function: _distributeFees (TitanX path)] After _distributeFees executes in the TitanX path, the approval 'IERC20(TITAN_X_ADDRESS).approve(address(buyAndProcess), costTitanX - requiredGenesisTitanX)' leaves a residual allowance of requiredTitanXBurn on buyAndProcess after distributeTitanXForBurning and distributeTitanXForBuilding consume only requiredBurnTitanX + requiredBuildTitanX. Does this residual allowance accumulate across multiple createTorus/stakeTorus calls, and can an unprivileged attacker exploit any buyAndProcess function that calls safeTransferFrom(torusCreateAndStake, ...) to drain TitanX from TorusCreateAndStake? Preconditions: multiple createTorus calls have been made with TitanX payment; residual allowance has accumulated. Call sequence: createTorus(power, days) x N -> attacker calls buyAndProcess.distributeTitanXForBurning(residualAmount) as msg.sender=attacker (not torusCreateAndStake). Invariant tested: TorusCreateAndStake's TitanX balance must not decrease except through _distributeFees execution. Scoped impact: TitanX drain from TorusCreateAndStake via accumulated allowance. Local proof idea: unit test asserting allowance(torusCreateAndStake, buyAndProcess) == 0 after each createTorus call completes.

[File: 'src/contracts/src/TorusCreateAndStake.sol -> Scope: Critical reward extraction or unfair reward access by an unprivileged attacker'] [Function: _checkAndUpdateRewardPool] When the for loop processes day d where totalShares[d]==0 and d != currentDay, it executes 'penaltiesInRewardPool[currentDay] += nextPool' and 'penaltiesInRewardPool[currentDay] += penaltiesInRewardPool[d]'. If penaltiesInRewardPool[d] was already non-zero from a previous earlyEndStake or late claim, is it possible for penaltiesInRewardPool[currentDay] to be double-counted: once when it was added to penaltiesInRewardPool[d] and again when rolled into penaltiesInRewardPool[currentDay], allowing stakers on currentDay to claim more than the total protocol reward allocation? Preconditions: earlyEndStake was called on day d-1, adding penalty to penaltiesInRewardPool[d]; no stakers on day d; _checkAndUpdateRewardPool called on day d+1. Call sequence: earlyEndStake(0) on day d-1 -> no interaction on day d -> createTorus on day d+1. Invariant tested: sum of all penaltiesInRewardPool[d] across all d must equal sum of all penalties deducted from claimants, never exceeding total protocol reward emissions. Scoped impact: stakers on currentDay extract more TORUS than the protocol emitted. Local proof idea: invariant fuzz test asserting cumulative penaltiesInRewardPool never exceeds cumulative penalty deductions.

[File: 'src/contracts/src/TorusCreateAndStake.sol -> Scope: Critical reward extraction or unfair reward access by an unprivileged attacker'] [Function: createTorus] Can an unprivileged attacker call createTorus with power=MAX_POWER=1000000 and lengthInDays=88 to mint an extremely large torusAmount = (BASE_FOR_88_DAYS_100_POWER_DAY1 * 88/88 * 1000000/100 * 1e18) / difficulty, then use _calculateShares(mintedTorus, 88) = mintedTorus * 88 * 88 to acquire a dominant share of totalShares[d] for 88 days, crowding out all other stakers and capturing the majority of rewardPool[d] for each day? Preconditions: attacker has sufficient TitanX (COST_100_POWER_TITANX * 1000000 / 100 = 1e30 TitanX) or ETH equivalent; protocol is in early days with low difficulty. Call sequence: approve(torusCreateAndStake, 1e30 TitanX) -> createTorus(1000000, 88). Invariant tested: no single staker should be able to acquire >99% of totalShares[d] for any day d through a single transaction. Scoped impact: attacker captures near-total rewardPool for 88 days, extracting rewards that should be distributed to all stakers. Local proof idea: fork test asserting that after createTorus(1000000, 88), attacker's shares / totalShares[d] < 0.99 for all d.

[File: 'src/contracts/src/TorusCreateAndStake.sol -> Scope: Critical reward extraction or unfair reward access by an unprivileged attacker'] [Function: earlyEndStake] In the reward calculation loop 'for (uint8 i = 0; i < daysElapsed; i++)', daysElapsed is capped at stakePos.stakingDays (uint24, max 88). The loop variable i is uint8 (max 255). If daysElapsed is computed as a uint256 and cast to uint8 via 'uint8(daysElapsed)' in the second loop, does the cast truncate correctly for all valid values, and is there any edge case where daysElapsed > 255 could cause the second loop to start from a wrong index, skipping share removal for some days and leaving stakePos.shares in totalShares[d] after the position is marked claimedStake=true? Preconditions: stakingDays=88; daysElapsed=88 (stake fully elapsed but endTime not yet reached). Call sequence: stakeTorus(amount, 88) -> warp 87 days 23 hours -> earlyEndStake(0). Invariant tested: after earlyEndStake, totalShares[d] must be reduced by stakePos.shares for every day d from daysElapsed to stakingDays-1. Local proof idea: unit test asserting totalShares[startDay+i] decreases by stakePos.shares for all i in [daysElapsed, stakingDays).

[File: 'src/contracts/src/TorusCreateAndStake.sol -> Scope: Critical reward extraction or unfair reward access by an unprivileged attacker'] [Function: claim] In the late-penalty branch, 'penaltiesInRewardPool[nextDay] += penalty / 2' adds only half the penalty to the next day's pool. The other half (penalty - penalty/2) is silently discarded (not burned, not redistributed). Does this create a systematic reward leak where late claimants' penalties are only 50% redistributed, and can an attacker exploit this by deliberately claiming late to minimize their own penalty while the discarded half reduces total rewards available to other stakers? Preconditions: attacker has a matured position; attacker waits GRACE_PERIOD + 1 day before claiming. Call sequence: createTorus(power, days) -> warp days + GRACE_PERIOD*86400 + 86400 -> claim(0). Invariant tested: penalty deducted from claimant must equal penalty added to penaltiesInRewardPool plus any burned amount; no value should be silently discarded. Scoped impact: systematic reward reduction for all stakers due to penalty half-burn. Local proof idea: unit test asserting penalty == penaltiesInRewardPool[nextDay] increase * 2.

[File: 'src/contracts/src/TorusCreateAndStake.sol -> Scope: Critical reward extraction or unfair reward access by an unprivileged attacker'] [Function: stakeTorus / _distributeFees] In the TitanX fee path of _distributeFees, the contract calls 'IERC20(TITAN_X_ADDRESS).safeTransferFrom(msg.sender, address(this), costTitanX)' to pull the full costTitanX, then transfers requiredGenesisTitanX to genesisWallet, approves buyAndProcess for costTitanX - requiredGenesisTitanX, calls distributeTitanXForBurning(requiredBurnTitanX) and distributeTitanXForBuilding(requiredBuildTitanX), and then safeTransfers requiredTitanXBurn to TITAN_X_BURN_ADDRESS. Does the sum requiredGenesisTitanX + requiredBurnTitanX + requiredBuildTitanX + requiredTitanXBurn equal costTitanX exactly (given integer division rounding), and if not, does the dust remain in TorusCreateAndStake and get sent to genesisWallet by the outer balance check? Preconditions: any createTorus or stakeTorus call with TitanX payment. Call sequence: createTorus(100, 1) with TitanX. Invariant tested: TorusCreateAndStake's TitanX balance must be zero after each createTorus/stakeTorus call (all TitanX distributed or refunded). Local proof idea: unit test asserting IERC20(TITAN_X_ADDRESS).balanceOf(torusCreateAndStake) == 0 after each call.

[File: 'src/contracts/src/TorusCreateAndStake.sol -> Scope: Critical reward extraction or unfair reward access by an unprivileged attacker'] [Function: createTorus / stakeTorus] In the ETH path of _distributeFees, the refund 'payable(msg.sender).call{value: refund, gas: 30000}' is executed before the outer balance check in createTorus/stakeTorus sends any remaining ETH to genesisWallet. If msg.sender is a contract whose receive() function re-enters createTorus or stakeTorus, does the nonReentrant guard on createTorus/stakeTorus prevent the re-entry, or is there a path through _distributeFees -> genesisWallet.call -> re-entry that bypasses the guard? Preconditions: attacker deploys a contract with a receive() that calls createTorus; sends ETH > requiredETH. Call sequence: attackerContract.attack{value: 2 ether}() -> createTorus(power, days) -> _distributeFees -> refund.call -> attackerContract.receive() -> createTorus(power, days). Invariant tested: nonReentrant must prevent any re-entry into createTorus or stakeTorus during _distributeFees execution. Scoped impact: re-entrant createTorus could mint TORUS without paying fees. Local proof idea: unit test asserting re-entrant createTorus call reverts with ReentrancyGuardReentrantCall.

[File: 'src/contracts/src/TorusCreateAndStake.sol -> Scope: Critical reward extraction or unfair reward access by an unprivileged attacker'] [Function: _checkAndUpdateRewardPool] If rewardPool[d] reduces to exactly 0 via FullMath.mulDiv(rewardPool[d-1], BPS_DENOM - DAILY_REDUCTION_RATE, BPS_DENOM) due to integer truncation (e.g., rewardPool[d-1] = 1 and BPS_DENOM - DAILY_REDUCTION_RATE = 9992), does the while loop 'while (lastFilled > 0 && rewardPool[lastFilled] == 0) { lastFilled--; }' incorrectly treat day d as unfilled and re-derive rewardPool[d] from rewardPool[d-1], overwriting the correct value of 0 with a potentially non-zero value, causing stakers on day d to receive rewards they should not? Preconditions: rewardPool has decayed to near-zero over many years; rewardPool[d-1] = 1. Call sequence: warp many years -> createTorus(power, 1) -> _checkAndUpdateRewardPool. Invariant tested: rewardPool[d] must be monotonically non-increasing and must not be re-derived after being set. Local proof idea: unit test with rewardPool[d-1]=1, assert rewardPool[d] is not overwritten on subsequent _checkAndUpdateRewardPool calls.

[File: 'src/contracts/src/TorusCreateAndStake.sol -> Scope: Critical reward extraction or unfair reward access by an unprivileged attacker'] [Function: claimBatch] In claimBatch, _checkAndUpdateRewardPool() is called once at the start, then calculateStakingRewards(stakePos) is called for each stake in the loop. If the batch contains stakes from different startDayIndex values, and rewardPool for some days was not populated by the single _checkAndUpdateRewardPool call (because those days are beyond currentDay), does calculateStakingRewards return 0 for those days, causing the attacker to receive fewer rewards than expected, or conversely, does it return stale non-zero values from a previous call? Preconditions: attacker has two positions: one starting on day 1 (matured) and one starting on day 50 (matured). Call sequence: createTorus on day 1 -> createTorus on day 50 -> warp to day 100 -> claimBatch([0, 1]). Invariant tested: claimBatch must produce identical rewards to calling claim() individually for each index. Local proof idea: differential test asserting claimBatch([0,1]) total mint == claim(0) + claim(1) on a forked state.

[File: 'src/contracts/src/TorusCreateAndStake.sol -> Scope: Critical reward extraction or unfair reward access by an unprivileged attacker'] [Function: earlyEndStake] The condition 'if (elapsed >= totalDuration) revert NotEligibleForEarlyClaim()' uses block.timestamp which can be manipulated by miners within ~15 seconds. Can a miner or validator manipulate block.timestamp to make elapsed appear to be just below totalDuration (e.g., totalDuration - 1 second), allowing earlyEndStake to succeed when it should revert, and claim partialRewards close to 100% of totalRewards while also removing shares from future days? Preconditions: attacker is a miner/validator; stake is at 99.999% elapsed. Call sequence: mine block with timestamp = stakePos.endTime - 1 -> earlyEndStake(0). Invariant tested: earlyEndStake must not be callable when elapsed >= totalDuration; claim() is the correct path at maturity. Scoped impact: attacker claims near-full rewards via earlyEndStake while also removing shares from future days (double benefit). Local proof idea: unit test with block.timestamp = endTime - 1, assert earlyEndStake succeeds and claimablePct = 98.

[File: 'src/contracts/src/TorusCreateAndStake.sol -> Scope: Critical reward extraction or unfair reward access by an unprivileged attacker'] [Function: calculateStakingRewards] The reward formula '(rewardPool[startDayIndex + i] * stakePos.shares) / totalShares[startDayIndex + i]' uses integer division which truncates. For a staker with shares = 1 and totalShares[d] = 1e30, the reward per day is 0. Can an attacker with a very large share count (via createTorus with MAX_POWER) cause totalShares[d] to be so large that all other stakers' per-day rewards round to 0, effectively stealing the entire rewardPool[d] for themselves? Preconditions: attacker calls createTorus(1000000, 88) acquiring dominant shares; victim has shares = 1. Call sequence: createTorus(1000000, 88) -> victim createTorus(1, 1) -> warp 1 day -> victim claim(0). Invariant tested: victim's reward must be >= 1 wei if victim's shares > 0 and rewardPool[d] > 0. Scoped impact: victim receives 0 rewards due to rounding, attacker captures full rewardPool. Local proof idea: unit test asserting victim reward > 0 when victim shares > 0 and rewardPool[d] > 0.

[File: 'src/contracts/src/TorusCreateAndStake.sol -> Scope: Critical reward extraction or unfair reward access by an unprivileged attacker'] [Function: _distributeFees (ETH path)] In the ETH path of _distributeFees, getEthAmountForTitanX(costTitanX) uses OracleLibrary.consult with secondsAgo = min(15*60, oldest). If the TitanX/ETH pool (0xc45a81bc23a64ea556ab4cdf08a86b61cdceea8b) has very few observations (oldest < 15*60), secondsAgo could be very small, making the TWAP highly manipulable within a single block. Can an attacker manipulate the TitanX/ETH spot price in the same block to make getEthAmountForTitanX return a very low ETH requirement, allowing createTorus/stakeTorus to proceed with insufficient ETH payment? Preconditions: TitanX/ETH pool has oldest observation < 60 seconds; attacker can move the pool price via a large swap. Call sequence: swap TitanX/ETH pool to inflate TitanX price -> createTorus(power, days){value: manipulated_low_ETH} -> swap back. Invariant tested: requiredETH must reflect fair market value of costTitanX regardless of short-term price manipulation. Scoped impact: attacker pays less ETH than the true cost of TitanX, extracting subsidized TORUS creation. Local proof idea: fork test with manipulated pool state, assert requiredETH >= true_market_value * 0.9.

[File: 'src/contracts/src/TorusCreateAndStake.sol -> Scope: Critical reward extraction or unfair reward access by an unprivileged attacker'] [Function: earlyEndStake] The penalty calculation 'penaltiesInRewardPool[todayIndex + 1] += (totalRewards - partialRewards) / 2' uses todayIndex = getCurrentDayIndex() which is computed after _checkAndUpdateRewardPool() is called. If an attacker calls earlyEndStake at the boundary between two days (block.timestamp just crosses into a new day), does todayIndex+1 point to a day that is currentDay+1 (future), and does this correctly add to the next day's penalty pool, or does it add to a day that has already been processed, causing the penalty to be uncollectable? Preconditions: earlyEndStake called at block.timestamp = protocolStart + N * 86400 (exact day boundary). Call sequence: stakeTorus(amount, 88) -> warp to exact day boundary -> earlyEndStake(0). Invariant tested: penaltiesInRewardPool[todayIndex+1] must be accessible to stakers whose stake spans day todayIndex+1. Local proof idea: unit test asserting penaltiesInRewardPool[todayIndex+1] > 0 after earlyEndStake and that a staker on day todayIndex+1 receives it.
